message:
refactor(wasm): retire standalone WASM client crate to experimental/ tree

Moves the `mcp-agent-mail-wasm` prototype out of the active Cargo
workspace and into `experimental/mcp-agent-mail-wasm/` per beads
track `br-il53l.12`. The retired crate is preserved as prior art,
not deleted — the source, HTML/JS/CSS surface, and original
`Cargo.toml` are all carried over byte-for-byte, and a new
`experimental/README.md` documents the parked status.

Why now
-------
The project's real browser surface is the server-rendered
`/web-dashboard` + `/mail/*` routes, which have their own HTML/JS
runtime and ship alongside the MCP server. The standalone WASM
client was an earlier attempt at a browser experience and has been
consistently under-covered relative to the dashboard. Keeping it as
a workspace member forced every `cargo check`, `cargo test`, and
release build to compile a WASM-targeted crate whose output nothing
downstream actually consumes, and it kept `console_error_panic_hook`
+ `wasm-bindgen`-only transitives on the lock file. Parking the
crate drops both costs without throwing the code away.

What changes
------------
- `crates/mcp-agent-mail-wasm/` — deleted (Cargo.toml, src/lib.rs,
  src/wasm.rs, www/index.html, www/index.js, www/style.css).
- `experimental/mcp-agent-mail-wasm/` — added, same files, same
  layout. The root workspace `Cargo.toml` already carries
  `exclude = ["experimental/*"]`, so no workspace-member edit is
  needed.
- `experimental/README.md` — new. Documents the "preservation, not
  shipment" rule: parked crates stay available as prior art, are
  excluded from workspace builds/releases, and may require
  `--manifest-path` when built directly. Includes the exact
  `cargo check --manifest-path experimental/mcp-agent-mail-wasm/
  Cargo.toml` invocation.
- `Cargo.lock` — 37 net-removed lines: the `mcp-agent-mail-wasm`
  package entry and its `console_error_panic_hook` transitive drop
  out now that the crate is no longer in the active workspace.
- `.gitignore` — adds `experimental/**/Cargo.lock` and
  `experimental/**/pkg/` so per-crate lockfiles and `wasm-pack`
  build output in the parked tree don't leak back into the active
  tree.
- `scripts/e2e_tui_wasm.sh` — the two existing cases that exercise
  the WASM crate (native unit tests, `wasm32-unknown-unknown`
  release build) now pass `--manifest-path "${WASM_MANIFEST}"`
  pointing at the experimental path, so the E2E harness keeps
  verifying the parked crate builds when someone explicitly runs it.

No behaviour change for the active server or CLI — the retired
surface was already unused outside its own E2E gate.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

@@ -0,0 +1,21 @@
+# Experimental Surfaces
+
+This directory holds parked or deferred code that is intentionally kept out of
+the active Cargo workspace.
+
+Current contents:
+
+- `mcp-agent-mail-wasm/`: the retired standalone WASM/browser client prototype
+  parked by `br-il53l.12`
+
+The goal is preservation, not shipment:
+
+- these crates stay available as prior art if the project revisits the idea
+- they are excluded from normal workspace build and release flows
+- they may require `--manifest-path` when built directly
+
+Example:
+
+```bash
+cargo check --manifest-path experimental/mcp-agent-mail-wasm/Cargo.toml
+```

@@ -1,12 +1,13 @@
 [package]
 name = "mcp-agent-mail-wasm"
-version.workspace = true
-edition.workspace = true
-license.workspace = true
-rust-version.workspace = true
-repository.workspace = true
-authors.workspace = true
-description = "WASM frontend for MCP Agent Mail TUI dashboard"
+version = "0.2.42"
+edition = "2024"
+license = "LicenseRef-MIT-Rider"
+rust-version = "1.95"
+repository = "https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/mcp_agent_mail_rust"
+authors = ["Jeff Emanuel"]
+publish = false
+description = "Parked experimental WASM frontend for MCP Agent Mail"
 
 [lib]
 # cdylib for WASM, rlib for native tests
@@ -22,8 +23,8 @@ console-panic = ["console_error_panic_hook"]
 # ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
 
 [dependencies]
-serde.workspace = true
-serde_json.workspace = true
+serde = { version = "1", features = ["derive"] }
+serde_json = { version = "1", features = ["preserve_order"] }
 
 # Core types (WASM-compatible subset)
 # Note: mcp-agent-mail-core has fs2/dirs deps that need conditional compilation
@@ -37,7 +38,7 @@ serde_json.workspace = true
 js-sys = "0.3.85"
 wasm-bindgen = "0.2.108"
 wasm-bindgen-futures = "0.4.58"
-serde-wasm-bindgen.workspace = true
+serde-wasm-bindgen = "0.6"
 console_error_panic_hook = { version = "0.1.7", optional = true }
 web-sys = { version = "0.3.85", features = [
     "Window",
@@ -68,7 +69,7 @@ web-time = "1.1.0"
 # ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
 
 [target.'cfg(not(target_arch = "wasm32"))'.dev-dependencies]
-tempfile.workspace = true
+tempfile = "3"
 
 # ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
 # WASM-pack optimization settings
@@ -81,5 +82,12 @@ tempfile.workspace = true
 # --converge: re-run optimization passes until output stabilizes
 wasm-opt = ["-Oz", "--all-features", "--converge"]
 
-[lints]
-workspace = true
+[lints.rust]
+unsafe_code = "forbid"
+
+[lints.clippy]
+pedantic = { level = "warn", priority = -1 }
+nursery = { level = "warn", priority = -1 }
+missing_errors_doc = "allow"
+missing_panics_doc = "allow"
+module_name_repetitions = "allow"

@@ -421,6 +421,7 @@ http_call_json() {
 
 BIN="$(resolve_binary)"
 e2e_log "using mcp-agent-mail binary: ${BIN}"
+WASM_MANIFEST="experimental/mcp-agent-mail-wasm/Cargo.toml"
 
 # ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
 # Case 1: WASM crate native unit tests (via rch)
@@ -430,7 +431,7 @@ scenario_diag_begin "wasm_native_unit_tests"
 CASE1_LOG="${E2E_ARTIFACT_DIR}/case_01_wasm_native_unit_tests.log"
 
 set +e
-run_cargo_with_rch_only "${CASE1_LOG}" test -p mcp-agent-mail-wasm --lib -- --nocapture
+run_cargo_with_rch_only "${CASE1_LOG}" test --manifest-path "${WASM_MANIFEST}" --lib -- --nocapture
 CASE1_RC=$?
 set -e
 CASE1_OUT="$(cat "${CASE1_LOG}" 2>/dev/null || true)"
@@ -458,7 +459,7 @@ scenario_diag_begin "wasm_target_build"
 CASE2_LOG="${E2E_ARTIFACT_DIR}/case_02_wasm_target_build.log"
 
 set +e
-run_cargo_with_rch_only "${CASE2_LOG}" build -p mcp-agent-mail-wasm --target wasm32-unknown-unknown --release
+run_cargo_with_rch_only "${CASE2_LOG}" build --manifest-path "${WASM_MANIFEST}" --target wasm32-unknown-unknown --release
 CASE2_RC=$?
 set -e
